🌅 Market News Digest
[Jul 21-22, 2026 EST]

🔥 Top Stories
• U.S.-Iran conflict escalates further — CENTCOM reports 11th straight night of strikes; Iran vows retaliation and reports attacks on U.S./allied bases, keeping geopolitics and shipping risk front and center
• Brent crude tops $95, oil spikes — Middle East supply fears and Hormuz disruption risks push oil to six-week highs
• Trump pushes sweeping generic-drug tariffs — 0% until Aug 2028, then 100% for a year and 200% after; major reshoring signal for pharma supply chains

⛽ Oil & Energy
• API showed a surprise 2.6M-barrel U.S. crude build; distillates/gasoline mixed, but crude still rallied on Iran risk
• Equinor beat on revenue and profit; Te Connectivity and GE Vernova also topped estimates with strong guidance/backlog
• U.S. aims to keep Hormuz open while degrading Iran’s shipping threat capability

📊 Markets & Macro
• UK June CPI eased to 2.6%, below forecast; core CPI held at 2.6%, reinforcing disinflation trend
• Japan trade deficit widened sharply as imports jumped 25.4% y/y; JGB yields climbed and yen weakened to 39-year lows
• South Korea CPI/PPI stayed hot; currency stability measures and exporter repatriation calls followed
• Asia equities rose on tech strength; U.S. futures slipped as investors weighed Iran risk and megacap earnings

🌍 Geopolitical
• Rubio said Iran is “not serious” about talks, while also leaving room for diplomacy and warning against blocking key waterways
• Iraq’s PM is set to visit Iran; Jordan shot down Iranian drones; reports said strikes hit sites in Iran, Kuwait and elsewhere in the region
• Saudi nuclear deal reportedly approved by Trump without safeguards, adding to regional strategic tension
• Ukraine war remained active with strikes on ports, energy assets and shipping infrastructure

🪙 Crypto & DeFi
• Balance Stablecoin collapsed after a $1M exploit drained its Bitcoin vaults
• Canton Digital Asset raised another $10M at a $2B valuation; Bitcoin ownership in the U.S. was estimated above gold ownership

🏛️ Regulatory & Policy
• White House plans to redirect billions in federal research funding toward AI, away from universities
• FCC is weighing a ban on imports of military-grade drones; U.S. also said it will cut aluminium tariffs to support defense supply chains
• Australia’s ACCC moved to implement the Google-Epic settlement; Hong Kong may offer hedge-fund tax breaks

🏢 Corporate
• Nvidia expanded beyond GPUs with Vera data-center CPUs; Wistron opened a Texas plant to make Nvidia superchips in the U.S.
• Intel says it remains committed to Ohio chipmaking; SK Hynix denies Ohio plant acquisition plans
• AT&T beat on wireless adds and EPS; Rocket Lab won a $266M U.S. Air Force contract; Brookfield Infrastructure announced simplification
• Moody’s upgraded Argentina to B3 with positive outlook; Santander, Iberdrola and Cathay Pacific delivered notable results
